title: realloc Semantics and Transactional Growth · Failure Case: Zero Realloc Policy
summary: A dynamic event list grows under memory pressure and loses its only pointer when resizing fails. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem zero realloc policy increment in amortized vector with failure invariants. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Avoid ambiguous zero-size realloc dependencies by handling logical empty state explicitly.
Define shrink success, failure, fragmentation, hysteresis, and old-value preservation rather than assuming space returns.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Zero Realloc Policy · Theory Zero Realloc Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 9451. Produce bounded C evidence to grow capacity without losing the old allocation on failure; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
